back_translation: While the two of them were speaking to the people, the priests, the captain of the temple guard, and the Sadducees came up to the two of them.
They felt extremely displeased, because the two of them were teaching the people and proclaiming the matter of the rising from the dead in Jesus.
So they arrested the two of them and took them to be held in custody until the next day, because that time was already evening.
But many who heard that word believed, and the number of men who believed grew to about five thousand.
The next day, the rulers, the elders, and the scribes of their people assembled in the city of Jerusalem,
including Annas who was the high priest, Caiaphas, John, Alexander, and all those descended from the family of the high priest.
They had the two of them stand in the middle and then examined them saying, 'By what power or in whose name did you do this?'
At that time Peter, full of the Holy Spirit, said to them, 'You rulers and elders of the people!
If we are being examined today because of a good deed done to a disabled man, (inquiring) by what means this man was made well to recover,

then let all of you and all the people of Israel know that by the name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth, whom you crucified on the cross, whom God raised up from the dead — by that name, this man stands before you in a healed condition.
This Jesus is 'the stone that you builders despised and discarded, which became the cornerstone.'
In no one else is there salvation, for there is no other name under heaven given among men by which we must be saved.
When they saw the boldness of Peter and John, and realized that the two of them were uneducated and ordinary men, they were amazed, and they recognized that the two of them had been with Jesus.
And when they saw the man who had been healed standing with the two of them, they had nothing they could argue against.
But when they ordered the two of them to leave the Sanhedrin council, they conferred among themselves,
saying, 'What shall we do with these men? For they have performed a notable sign, evident to everyone living in the city of Jerusalem, and we cannot deny it.
But so that this matter does not spread any more among the people, let us threaten them not to speak in this name to anyone anymore.'
Then they called the two of them in and commanded them not to speak or teach in the name of Jesus at all.
But Peter and John answered them, 'Consider for yourselves whether obeying you rather than obeying God is right in the sight of God,
because we cannot not speak about what we have seen and heard.'

When they had threatened the two of them again, they released the two of them, being unable to find a way to punish them, because they feared the people, since everyone was giving glory to God for what had happened.
For the man upon whom this sign of healing had occurred was more than forty years old.
When they had been released, the two of them went back to their own people and told everything that the chief priests and elders had said to the two of them.

When they heard this, they with one accord lifted up their voices praying to God, 'O Sovereign Lord of supreme authority, you are the one who made the heavens, the earth, the sea, and all things that are in them,
you spoke by the Holy Spirit through the mouth of David, our ancestor and your servant, saying, \u2039Why did the nations rage with fury, and the peoples devise useless things?
The kings of the earth took their battle stand, and the rulers gathered together, to oppose the Lord and the One whom he has anointed.\u203a
For truly both Herod and Pontius Pilate, together with the Gentiles and the peoples of Israel, assembled in this city to oppose Jesus your holy servant, whom you anointed,
in order to do all that your hand and your purpose had predetermined to happen.
Now, O Lord, please look upon their threats, and grant to your servants to speak your word with all boldness,
by stretching out your hand to heal and to cause signs and wonders to occur in the name of Jesus your holy servant.'
When they had finished praying, the place where they were gathered shook, and everyone was filled with the Holy Spirit, and they spoke the word of God with boldness.
Now the great number of believers were of one heart and one mind, and no one claimed any of their possessions as their own private property, but everything was held in common among them.
The apostles testified to the resurrection of the Lord Jesus with great power, and great grace was upon them all.
For among them no one lacked anything, because all who owned lands or houses sold them and brought the money received from the sales,
and laid it at the feet of the apostles, and it was distributed to everyone according to what each one needed.
Now Joseph, whom the apostles renamed Barnabas (which is translated 'son of encouragement'), was of the tribe of Levi, born on the island of Cyprus.
He sold his field, brought the money, and laid it at the feet of the apostles.
